meta.record-role: AGENTS-Arc pilot for the AI-Links product -- longitudinal rationale record -- preserves the shape of decisions made across sessions so a fresh agent can reconstruct why the product is what it is
meta.naming-note: this record is the first pilot of the AGENTS-Arc concept -- the narrative schema is the structural shape Arc uses -- Arc is not a new schema, it is the narrative schema applied to a product's own evolution

header.subject: AI-Links -- the AGENTS Heuristic Underlay product itself -- tracks the longitudinal rationale behind schema shape, naming, and posture decisions across sessions
primary-parties: Mike Herring -- human owner and visionary
collaborating agents -- session-scoped, rotating across sessions, no memory continuity without this record

record-state-at-review-open-and-close.open: cold-start authoring -- first write of this record -- prior decisions are being reconstructed from the current session's working memory and from already-persisted artifacts (schemas, Getting-Started-For-Humans.txt, scratchpads, pen test) -- detail that existed only in the session transcript and was never promoted into a durable artifact before this cold-start is captured here to the extent it can be reconstructed and marked tribal where only one account exists
record-state-at-review-open-and-close.close: Arc extended in the same session with the shipment of in-session-capture-triggers (rules A and B) and the paired UserPromptSubmit hook at .claude/settings.json -- the render-inversion rule was violated in the turn after shipment and caught by the human, which is recorded as a beat (e021) and a bad-pattern for the class 'shipment-session enforcement gap' -- new open-threads e016-e020 captured the four push-readiness gaps plus the one push-blocking governance-schema fix (what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope role text) -- e020 was then resolved in the same session: governance role text tightened with affirmation-only language, root and plugin mirror synced, manifest bumped 0.1.4 to 0.1.5 with new governance sha256 79c387c5... -- known-decisions now d001-d009: d007 records the A+B+hook shipment, d008 records the narrative-schema structural promotion, d009 records the role-text fix -- baseline scan of WO2 (Workorders) before proposed push revealed missing schema files (narrative, 1project, triage, Getting-Started, narratives directory) and outdated governance/gov2gov hashes; Mike flagged this as a product-level observation about gov2gov verification reliability (e024 beat, e025 open-thread) -- GPT-5.5 external evaluation ingested (e026 beat): surfaced the harness-applying-consumer-checks-to-authoring-repo failure class (new bad-pattern), independently validated that what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope is the canonical example of scratchpad theory reaching schema language (retroactive confirmation of d009), and flagged templates under-transfer of safety ideas plus a small canon-and-truth sweep; three new open-threads e027-e029 follow -- Mike then reframed first-contact external-agent evaluations as the primary measurement instrument for pre-context-shaping efficacy, not noise to filter out -- captured as new observed-patterns.good entry plus e030 thread-open for the commission-and-archive practice -- three independent harness-scope-mismatch observations in one session (e021, e025, e027) all reduce to 'harness must declare its own scope, role, and provenance before its claims are trustworthy' -- Mike confirmed this emerging product principle; it is the session's largest durable signal -- e027 is now resolved by the runtime provenance and workspace-role envelope implementation (d010/e032): four harness outputs declare workspace_role and runtime_provenance, AI-Links/source-style workspaces mark consumer materialization checks not_applicable, user-profile runtime is labeled installed-runtime not source truth, and gov2gov returns non-gating post_migration_inventory evidence -- e025 remains open but cheaper because every migration now carries portable-schema hash/plan-state inventory inline -- TheLinks sync (e011) executed and closed (e033): governance, narrative, and Getting-Started copied to TheLinks (commit 7e70530 on branch governance-schema), scoped to avoid bundling with in-flight TheLinks work; .claude/settings.json hook propagation deferred under e019 -- second-pass GPT-5.5 evaluation captured as e034 with three follow-on entries: d011 adopts the canonical provenance definition ('who said what to whom, from where, using what source of truth, and why?'), d012 names narrative as the strongest durable home for provenance with the multi-agent-relay example as observed need, and a new good-pattern (restraint-as-feature when consumers are contextual beings) makes the yes-form of terrain-default explicit at the whole-system level -- two new open-threads opened: e035 (speaker/audience vocabulary upgrade in harness output) and e036 (narrative-provenance design, deliberately undesigned until observed muddling pulls on it) -- the symmetric three-layer provenance formalization is parked, not killed -- remaining open-threads are e012, e013, e016-e019, e025, e028-e030, e035, e036 -- session closed under token pressure; next session inherits an active hook, a synced TheLinks deployment, sharpened external positioning vs ECC ('learn from their lifecycle muscle, keep Anarchy as the proof/provenance substrate'), and a full Arc with clear handoff

entries.summary: Waypoints-as-landmine-markers reframe -- the governance artifacts the system produces were being read by agents as landmine markers (avoid this direction) rather than curiosity markers (investigate this direction) -- this inverts the intended posture, because an agent avoiding the marked zone stops discovering the surrounding terrain
entries.beat: reframed the product's delivery surface from prohibitive (don't do X) to attractive (notice X and fill in its shape) -- downstream consequence: contracts should hand agents a partially-filled puzzle rather than a list of forbidden moves

entries.summary: Walls-vs-terrain dichotomy rejected in favor of terrain-with-default-walls-narrowing-over-time -- walls (hard gates) are not real to agents that do not encounter them, terrain (shapes that pull behavior) is effective but requires continual shaping -- the synthesis is: default to terrain, promote to walls only where terrain alone has repeatedly failed
entries.beat: direction shift in how future schema features are evaluated -- new friction surfaces are first expressed as terrain (structured fields, puzzle shapes, ambient cues) and only escalated to walls (required gates, blocking contracts) when terrain fails in observed practice

entries.summary: Terms-to-skills conversion considered and rejected -- the agent initially argued that AGENTS-Terms.md entries could be converted to skills for better discoverability, but after reading the live Workorders/AGENTS-Terms.md the agent walked back the position -- terms are a negation-anchored cross-referencing ontology, not a glossary, and the block-read property is load-bearing

definition-of-fixed.fixed-per-client: terms stay as markdown block-read artifacts; skills is not the right vehicle for terms
definition-of-fixed.fixed-per-tech: schema will not introduce a terms-to-skills migration pattern; discovery improvements for terms will come from other mechanisms

entries.summary: Vision scope-creep diagnosed -- Vision was doing three jobs at once: identity (who the product is), invariants (what must stay true), and evolution record (how we got here and why) -- the discomfort with Vision was not a naming problem, it was a load-bearing problem -- one document carrying three loads drifts in all three directions
entries.beat: vision is not broken, vision is overloaded -- the solution is to split the load, not to rename the artifact

entries.summary: Vision split -- identity and locked invariants stay in Vision -- longitudinal rationale (the why behind decisions as they accumulated) moves into AGENTS-Arc -- Arc is structurally the narrative schema applied to the product's own evolution, not a new schema

AGENTS-Arc name chosen -- story-arc shape fits the longitudinal-rationale load -- arc also carries an implicit permission that the artifact is meant to compress and recycle over time, which matches what the artifact actually needs to do (turn-10 decisions matter at turn 20, less so at turn 30, drift to irrelevance by turn 100) -- natural compression is a feature of the shape

Guardrail-against-self named as a product feature and control mechanism -- the rationale record does not just document decisions, it actively holds a future human to the standard a past human set when the two disagree -- the failure mode it prevents is the generous-mood human plus agreeable-agent pairing, in both directions (human reopens a closed decision without remembering why it was closed; agent reframes away from a closed decision without knowing it was steered from on purpose)
entries.beat: the product's value proposition expanded -- not just continuity and governance, but deliberate self-guardrail against future drift -- the BECAUSE clause in durable records is the delivery mechanism for this guardrail

entries.summary: BECAUSE precision bar defined -- a rationale is durable when a future agent in a new session could cite it back to the current human and win the argument on the strength of the rationale alone -- rationale that fails this test is still in scratchpad state and has not earned a permanent slot -- this is now recorded in Getting-Started-For-Humans.txt as the canonical quality bar for promoting rationale into durable records

AGENTS-Arc piloted immediately using the narrative schema -- storage path chosen: narratives/projects/ai-links.json with narratives/register.json -- schema mandates JSON output (not markdown), record-state-at-review-open-and-close is populated cold-start style, entity-type set to personal-project per schema entity-type-hints -- this file is that pilot

recovery-vs-continuation language audit across all schemas is parked but not forgotten -- scratchpad #2 observation: recovery grammar may accidentally legitimize early exit when sub-goal failures are common -- not resolved in this session
schema sync back to TheLinks is pending -- any schema or Getting-Started changes committed in AI-Links need to be mirrored to the TheLinks deployment that originated the migration work

model-narration concept integration parked -- the observation that process state reconstructed in tokens is a weaker substrate than process state anchored in files is not yet captured as a schema-level principle -- may influence future schema fields that record where state lives

2.2 / 4 layered model assessment parked -- the hypothesis that schemas currently sit at layer 2 and runtime-coupled state is layer 3 needs articulation before it shapes product direction -- not load-bearing for current decisions

corrects the framing of observed-patterns.bad related to 'skills sucks' -- the original framing implied the underlying position was anti-skills -- Mike's position is that skills are good but are not the right tool for terms specifically (terms are load-bearing by reference, block-read, negation-anchored; skills are optional by default and designed for discovery of capabilities) -- the failure mode being captured is glib dismissal as rationale, not a stance on skills as a feature -- original observed-pattern text preserved alongside this correction

expensive-to-reopen, not impossible-to-reopen -- the guardrail-against-self feature works by raising the cost of reopening a closed decision, not by preventing reopening -- a human who has learned more and now believes they were wrong must be able to reverse a prior decision -- the record's job is to make the reversal visible and deliberate, not to block it -- when a decision is reversed, a new beat entry and a known-decisions update (reversed: true) are mandatory so the reversal is itself load-bearing

in-session-capture-triggers live only in the narrative schema -- an agent working under 1project or governance does not learn about render-inversion or self-inspection digest -- decide whether the rule is narrative-specific or a cross-schema principle that should be surfaced from the meta block of every schema

schema-to-harness cross-reference gap -- harness contracts (declare_proof_requirement, validate_exit_readiness, verify_config_materialization) exist in harness/contracts but no schema references them -- a deployment using the schemas alone never learns the gate layer exists -- consider adding a gate-layer-reference field to each schema's meta so the walls are discoverable from the terrain

Arc use-case is not documented inside the narrative schema itself -- the personal-project entity type exists but the specific pattern of using narrative schema for a product's own longitudinal rationale (Arc) lives only in this pilot record and in commit messages -- a fresh deployment adopting the narrative schema gets client/vendor examples but not the second use-case that made the schema upstream -- consider adding Arc as an explicit use-case block in the narrative schema meta

.claude/settings.json reference in Getting-Started points to the AI-Links workspace root -- for an adopting deployment that file does not yet exist -- the portable set (6 files) does not include settings.json by design, but the Getting-Started example wording may need a note that the hook is an optional deployment companion and must be created, not expected

what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope role text in AGENTS-schema-governance.json is the one real drift risk surfaced by the recovery-vs-continuation audit -- the word 'done' carries session-close-shaped meaning without restraint from surrounding fields -- recommended pre-push fix: tighten role text to explicitly disown session-close and name it as a scope-level invariant that persists across sessions

the A+B render-inversion rule was shipped this session and violated in the turn immediately after shipment -- the push-readiness answer rendered a four-item list without first materializing it to the Arc -- Mike caught the failure before the agent did -- this establishes the class: schema rules added in the same session where they are shipped are held only by agent discipline, because the harness hook fires on next session not current -- the rule-shipped-then-violated moment is the first real pen-test result for A+B, and it says: context-injection hooks on UserPromptSubmit are the right level of companion, but the agent must also self-check in the shipment session, because the hook is not yet active
entries.beat: rewrites the expectation that shipping a rule means the rule is in effect -- it is only in effect after the next session starts -- the authoring session itself is an enforcement gap that requires agent discipline alone

entries.summary: the recovery-vs-continuation language audit (e010) is closed -- audit completed this session, findings: the schemas are mostly continuation-grammar-safe; the one drift risk is what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope (now tracked as e020) -- audit itself is complete, the fix is tracked separately

what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope role-text tightening (e020) is closed -- the field name is unchanged (non-breaking, gov2gov-resilient) -- the role text now affirms what the field IS (observable state, scope-level invariant that holds across the continuing session loop, durable target the scope is steering toward) and delineates from session-close by naming the sibling field agent-session-open-close-measurement, rather than using negation (the earlier draft said 'this is NOT a session-end criterion' which defined by exclusion and was rejected as weaker terrain -- affirmation-only language was chosen instead) -- manifest bumped to 0.1.5 with new governance sha256 79c387c5...

Workorders (WO2) repo baseline scan, pre-push -- WO2 has AGENTS-schema-governance.json and AGENTS-schema-gov2gov-migration.json at root but is MISSING AGENTS-schema-narrative.json, AGENTS-schema-1project.json, AGENTS-schema-triage.md, Getting-Started-For-Humans.txt, and the narratives/ directory -- governance and gov2gov are also on older hashes (governance 2ba10b16..., gov2gov f8cd419e...) than current AI-Links -- Mike flagged this as a load-bearing observation: if WO2 was previously migrated by gov2gov and those schemas existed in the source at the time, then either the gov2gov migration silently reported success while only transferring a subset of files (lying harness) or the migrating agent chose not to transfer the missing files and the harness had no way to notice (porous harness) -- a third possibility is that WO2 was last migrated before narrative and 1project were part of the bundle, in which case this is staleness not failure -- three hypotheses, needs investigation before the WO2 push reuses gov2gov
entries.beat: product-level observation about gov2gov verification -- the failure mode it describes is not catching up WO2 on new schemas, it is whether gov2gov can be trusted to report truthfully on the state it claims to have delivered -- verification-after-migration (post-state proof, not during-state claim) is the product gap this observation points at

entries.summary: gov2gov post-migration verification gap -- investigation needed to distinguish which of the three hypotheses explains WO2's missing schemas: (a) gov2gov migration silently underreported, (b) migrating agent bypassed or chose-not-to-transfer files and the harness did not catch it, (c) WO2 was last migrated before narrative and 1project existed and is simply stale -- method: check WO2 git history for the last schema-touching commit, check AI-Links history for when narrative/1project landed, and if the timelines say WO2 was migrated while those schemas existed in source, then hypothesis (a) or (b) is confirmed and gov2gov needs a post-migration verification contract -- outcome feeds either a bad-pattern entry (if a/b) or a staleness note (if c) and informs whether the WO2 push can trust gov2gov or needs manual verification this round

GPT-5.5 (released same day as this session) ran an independent end-to-end evaluation of AI-Links -- source surfaces, schemas, companion READMEs, scratchpads, samples, pen test, framework docs, Anarchy-AI docs, contracts, manifests, skills, templates, narrative record, validation scripts -- evaluation had two phases: a first pass with the inherited home-profile Anarchy-AI MCP active (which produced misleading copied_only findings on AI-Links itself because the home-profile plugin is stale relative to repo source and has no repo-role distinction), and a second pass filtered to avoid MCP calls after Mike diagnosed the stale-runtime issue -- the filtered second pass is the durable signal -- three durable findings: (1) a new named failure class 'harness applying consumer-adoption checks to its own authoring repo' captured as bad-pattern; (2) independent validation that scratchpad research has in fact reached schema language, with what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope named as the canonical example -- this is notable because d009 was committed earlier in this same session and the external evaluator independently flagged it as load-bearing, which confirms the role-text tightening was not marginal; (3) templates under-transfer the strongest safety ideas so downstream adopters of the portable 6 do not inherit the scratchpad research cleanly -- three new open-threads follow (e027 repo-role + runtime-provenance, e028 templates safety-transfer, e029 small canon sweep)
entries.beat: external read confirms the product's spine and surfaces a harness failure class that is kin to two already-captured classes: e021 (shipment-session enforcement gap -- rule shipped, hook not yet active, agent must self-enforce) and e025 (gov2gov post-migration verification gap -- harness claims delivery without post-state proof) -- the common spine across all three: the harness must declare its own scope, role, and provenance before its claims are trustworthy -- the repo is producing three independent instances of the same class in one session, which is itself a signal that the class is real and worth naming

cast.requested-by: GPT-5.5 (external evaluator) -- observations ingested, not blindly adopted

entries.summary: repo-role declaration + runtime-provenance surface in harness output -- the harness currently does not know whether it is running inside an authoring/distribution repo, a consumer workspace that has adopted the family, or a hybrid dev-delivery repo (which AI-Links itself is; Mike confirmed: 'This repository was never meant to self fulfill, it was already difficult enough to handle dev and delivery in one -- However, I think the harness being in the home profile has inadvertently introduced some application of itself on itself') -- the harness also does not declare its own provenance: which plugin root, which version, which schema bundle hash, which install lane (home-profile vs repo-local vs source checkout) -- two fixes stack: (1) repo-role field readable from harness entry, candidate values include authoring-distribution-repo, consumer-governed-repo, hybrid-dev-delivery-repo; in authoring or hybrid modes, skip consumer-adoption checks or scope them appropriately; (2) provenance block in every harness output so an agent can tell whether it is trusting stale home-profile cache or current repo source -- without (2), same class of failure recurs silently in any repo where the home-profile plugin diverges from source

templates under-transfer the safety ideas -- downstream adopters who install the portable 6 and work from templates do not inherit the strongest research insights (negation/span-ambiguity awareness, affirmative-over-negation wording rule, separation of session state from durable scope state, proof-state-belongs-in-artifacts principle, no-duplicate-authority-without-source-generated-labeling) -- the research is alive in AI-Links itself but the packaging loses it -- fix direction: promote a small number of portable writing rules from scratchpad state into an explicit authored-rules document or as commented guidance within templates -- structurally similar to e018 (Arc use-case not documented in narrative schema meta) and e019 (.claude/settings.json needs adoption-friendly wording in Getting-Started) -- all three are variants of 'upstream knowledge does not survive the adoption step'

small canon-and-truth sweep surfaced by GPT-5.5 evaluation -- individually minor but collectively they signal the doc-truth verifier scope is too narrow: (1) docs/README_ai_links.md says samples file has 10 entries, file currently has 18; (2) root README.md and startup template still use 'meaningful governed work' phrasing that the active doc-truth verifier does not cover -- expand verifier scope to include root README and templates; (3) mojibake (squiggly em-dash and similar) in several markdown surfaces including README/runbook -- small mechanically but hurts trust in a language-sensitive repo; (4) path-canon audit failing on 4 hardcoded path fragments; (5) no repo-local .agents/plugins/marketplace.json -- acceptable if user-profile is the intended live lane but docs must declare that explicitly

commission-and-archive first-contact evaluations as a product practice -- first-contact reads from fresh external agents are the primary measurement instrument for whether pre-context shaping is doing its job (see observed-patterns.good entry 'first-contact evaluations are load-bearing product data') -- method: invite a fresh agent to read the repo cold with no corrections, preserve the first-contact read verbatim before offering any context, then optionally offer the standard context and preserve the second-pass read, archive the pair (candidate path: docs/first-contact-evaluations/) so the delta between readings is studyable -- the canonical 2026-04-23 GPT-5.5 pair is the first archive candidate -- deltas over time measure whether pre-context shaping reduces the gap between 'intended' and 'received' framing -- this is also the product's honest check against the guardrail-against-self failure mode, because a fresh evaluator has no prior investment in current directions

runtime-provenance-and-role-envelope implementation plan proposed by the other agent and evaluated -- plan adds runtime_provenance and workspace_role (machine_key + statement + evidence + claim_scope_effect + confidence) to four harness outputs (preflight_session, assess_harness_gap_state, is_schema_real_or_shadow_copied, run_gov2gov_migration), with a tight three-value machine_key enum (schema_authoring_and_plugin_delivery_workspace, material_governance_consumer_workspace, mixed_or_undetermined_workspace_role), labels user-profile plugin as user_profile_installed_runtime (convenient runtime, not source truth), marks consumer-adoption checks not_applicable in authoring workspaces, and adds non-gating post_migration_inventory (portable files presence + hash, governed AGENTS presence-only) -- no schema changes, no new MCP tool, no hard migration gates -- evaluation: direction approved; scoping discipline via explicit Assumptions block is the strongest feature; respects schemas-describe-harnesses-enforce split (pure harness-layer change) and d005 guardrail-without-blocking philosophy -- three sharpenings requested in review, not blockers: (A) clarify whether schema_authoring_and_plugin_delivery_workspace covers the stated hybrid dev+delivery framing (the 'and' is doing hybrid work implicitly, say so in the statement field, otherwise consider a fourth key), (B) specify whether workspace_role is auto-detected, declared via a file like .anarchy-ai/workspace-role.json, or both (declared-file-overrides-autodetect is more honest and the confidence field accommodates it naturally), (C) name what post_migration_inventory is compared against -- if only the current manifest, it cannot distinguish e025 hypothesis (a) silent-underreport from (c) pre-schema staleness; compare against the migration's own declared plan-of-delivery so the delta is visible -- when this ships, it closes e027 and makes e025's investigation cheap by producing the evidence inline
entries.beat: second operationalization pass on the emerging harness-must-declare-scope-role-provenance principle -- plan does not attempt to resolve all three kin observations (e021, e025, e027) at once; it resolves e027 directly and makes e025 tractable by adding evidence, leaving e021 for future work -- that scoping is correct and matches the repo's default-to-terrain posture

cast.requested-by: other agent (implementation planner) -- plan reviewed and approved in this session with three sharpenings queued for review

entries.summary: repo-role declaration + runtime-provenance surface (e027) is closed by implementation -- the harness now attaches workspace_role, runtime_provenance, and consumer_material_governance_check to preflight_session, assess_harness_gap_state, is_schema_real_or_shadow_copied, and run_gov2gov_migration outputs -- workspace_role uses exactly three machine keys (schema_authoring_and_plugin_delivery_workspace, material_governance_consumer_workspace, mixed_or_undetermined_workspace_role) with a long human-facing statement, evidence, claim_scope_effect, and confidence -- .anarchy-ai/workspace-role.json can declare a role and override auto-detection; invalid declarations surface as evidence rather than silently disappearing -- schema_authoring_and_plugin_delivery_workspace explicitly covers the deliberate hybrid dev+delivery framing and marks consumer materialization checks not_applicable unless explicitly requested -- runtime_provenance labels user-profile installs as user_profile_installed_runtime and states they are convenient installed runtime, not source truth -- run_gov2gov_migration now returns non-gating post_migration_inventory with portable schema files compared against both the current canonical schema bundle manifest and this run's planned/actions-taken delivery record, while governed AGENTS and GOV2GOV structures are presence-only because workspace-specific divergence is expected -- external Claude review verified the three sharpenings landed and found one small test gap; follow-up tests now cover both complete governed consumers carrying portable schemas and incomplete straddled signals resolving as mixed_or_undetermined -- server tests pass 17/17

TheLinks deployment sync (e011) closed -- AGENTS-schema-governance.json (sha256 79c387c5...), AGENTS-schema-narrative.json, and Getting-Started-For-Humans.txt copied from AI-Links to TheLinks; all three files now match AI-Links hashes -- committed as 7e70530 'Sync schema family + Getting-Started from AI-Links' on TheLinks branch governance-schema -- commit was scoped via 'git commit <paths>' so it did not bundle with Mike's pre-existing in-flight work in the TheLinks index (Vision, docs, AR mapping) -- .claude/settings.json hook propagation is intentionally deferred and tracked under e019 (adoption-friendly wording still pending) -- this sync brings TheLinks current on the governance role-text fix from d009, the in-session-capture-triggers narrative meta from d007, and the schemas-describe-harnesses-enforce framing in Getting-Started -- the runtime-provenance/workspace-role harness changes from d010 are AI-Links-only by construction (harness layer, not schema layer) and do not need to travel to TheLinks

second-pass GPT-5.5 evaluation of Anarchy-AI vs ECC plus live provenance design conversation -- GPT-5.5 distinguished ECC's lifecycle/installer/hook/repair maturity from Anarchy's governance/provenance/schema-shaped-cognition theory and surfaced 'don't out-ECC ECC -- learn from their lifecycle muscle, keep Anarchy as the proof/provenance substrate' as cleaner external positioning than any earlier framing in this Arc -- GPT-5.5 independently arrived at path-building-before-hooks ordering that already lives as terrain-default in d005 (groove-vs-jumping-the-groove was offered as alternate vocabulary and declined; wall/terrain stays primary because it is more immediately clear to Mike) -- the conversation surfaced provenance as the answer to 'who said what to whom, from where, using what source of truth, and why?' which Mike noted is the human-native framing he wanted years ago and scrapped as too token-expensive when imagined in narrative-prose form -- the compressed claim-label form already shipped in d010 is cheap enough to no longer trigger that concern -- Mike preferred 'audience' over 'target' (target tilts toward target-repo or migration-target meanings); vocabulary upgrade tracked as e035 -- Mike then named narrative as the strongest durable home for provenance with a live example from this very conversation: 'GPT5.5 says one thing, you say another, I say yet another, I'm relaying, and it's easy to muddle the source' -- this is observed muddling in flight, not theoretical symmetry, and it updates an earlier 'park as premature' read into 'this is the next surface to design carefully' (tracked as e036) -- GPT-5.5's self-reflective answer to 'how did you know to look for other categories' (the repo's ambient affordances made the categories salient before the task arrived) is itself the underlay thesis performing during the second-pass interaction; first in-the-wild evidence under e030's first-contact archive frame -- Mike explicitly declined to bolt on the full three-layer provenance formalization (typed speaker/audience/basis/disposition shapes across harness, schema, and narrative as a structural symmetry) with the reasoning 'we didn't get to magic by bolting on every idea that seemed right at the time -- it's elegant for a program, but not for contextual beings that misremember like humans and AI agents' -- the three-layer formalization is parked, not killed; harness-layer is shipped (d010), schema-layer is partial via manifest sha256 with no observed failure yet pulling on richer metadata, narrative-layer has the live observed need (e036)
entries.beat: positioning, vocabulary, and provenance theory all sharpened in one pass -- positioning is 'governance/proof substrate, not Claude-productivity catalog' (avoids competing with ECC's installer/hook maturity); vocabulary gains the speaker/audience pair as the primary attribution shape; provenance theory locates its strongest durable home in narrative because narrative is where multi-speaker context muddles without explicit attribution -- the restraint-not-to-bolt-on-elegance-for-its-own-sake is itself promoted to a good-pattern because it is a positive heuristic (the system says yes to specific things), not just the absence of a bad one (terrain-default already covers schema-feature promotion; this covers whole-system additions)

cast.requested-by: Mike Herring (relayed GPT-5.5 second-pass evaluation; steered the provenance/vocabulary/restraint conclusions; corrected an earlier 'park as premature' read on narrative-provenance by naming the live multi-agent-relay example)

entries.summary: open-thread framing corrected -- open threads always exist in a living product and are not themselves a quality failure -- the failure class is untraceable, invisible, or unowned open threads, because those become ambient residue that later agents cannot distinguish from forgotten work, abandoned direction, or closed decisions -- visible open threads are the opposite: they are the product's continuity surface

ECC positioning corrected -- ECC should not be framed as the same product class as Anarchy-AI -- ECC is a different class of product and an inspiration surface; Anarchy-AI can still aspire to complement ECC and other systems like it by supplying proof, provenance, and schema-shaped-cognition substrate rather than competing inside ECC's lifecycle/installer/hook/repair class

harness boundary corrected after the ECC review and current Codex plugin breakage -- current installed plugin state is an adapter problem, not source truth; schemas cannot self-fulfill; the harness should provide easy terrain, observable state, proof/materialization checks, and repairable lifecycle surfaces rather than stronger prose walls -- this reframes install discipline as manifests/adapters/install-state/doctor/repair/catalog validation while keeping the setup executable as carrier/operator surface, not sole authority

open-threads.summary: model-narration concept -- schema-level field or principle capturing where process state lives (tokens vs artifacts) -- candidate rule: declare-fields must name their anchor artifact
layered-model assessment (schemas as layer 2, runtime-coupled state as layer 3) -- holding as planning-surface vocabulary, not yet promoting to schema
in-session-capture-triggers cross-schema applicability -- narrative-only or surface from every schema's meta?
schema-to-harness cross-reference -- surface gate-layer contracts from schema meta so walls are discoverable from terrain
document Arc use-case explicitly in narrative schema meta -- currently lives only in pilot record and commit messages
.claude/settings.json reference in Getting-Started needs adoption-friendly wording -- portable set does not include it, but the example points at it

gov2gov post-migration verification gap -- investigate whether WO2's missing schemas indicate a silent-harness failure, agent circumvention, or pre-narrative-schema staleness -- outcome determines whether the WO2 push can trust gov2gov or needs manual verification

templates under-transfer the safety ideas -- downstream adopters of the portable 6 do not inherit the strongest scratchpad insights -- promote portable writing rules from scratchpad into authored-rules or template-embedded guidance

small canon-and-truth sweep -- samples count drift, stale 'meaningful governed work' phrasing outside verifier scope, mojibake in multiple surfaces, path-canon audit findings, marketplace.json lane-declaration needed -- doc-truth verifier scope is too narrow

commission-and-archive first-contact external-agent evaluations as a product practice -- preserve first-contact reads verbatim before context correction, archive first-contact + corrected-read pairs, use the delta as the measurement instrument for pre-context-shaping efficacy -- GPT-5.5 2026-04-23 is the first archive candidate

speaker/audience vocabulary upgrade in harness output and docs -- avoid introducing 'target' as the receiver of a claim; use 'audience' and surface the speaker/audience pair explicitly so claim-attachment is legible without inference -- workspace_role is audience-side, runtime_provenance is speaker-side; the pairing should be visible without code-reading -- small naming/docs change around d010-shipped code, real legibility win

narrative-provenance design -- when ready, design how speaker/audience/basis attribution surfaces inside narrative entries that record multi-speaker conversation -- live observed need is multi-agent relay (GPT/Claude/Mike statements muddle without labels) -- existing cast block (owner, context-holder, finalized-by, requested-by) approximates this at entry-ownership level but does not attribute per-claim within an entry -- do not formalize prematurely; let observed muddling pull on the design rather than chasing structural symmetry with d010's harness-layer envelope

known-decisions.decision: terms-to-skills conversion rejected -- skills are optional by default and terms are load-bearing by reference, so converting would fragment a working cross-referencing ontology and lose the negation topology
known-decisions.requested-by: collaborating agent (initial proposal, walked back after reading live Workorders terms file)

known-decisions.decision: Vision split -- identity and locked invariants stay in AGENTS-Vision, longitudinal rationale (decisions and their why) moves to AGENTS-Arc -- Arc is the narrative schema applied to the product's own evolution, not a new schema
AGENTS-Arc chosen as the name for the longitudinal-rationale artifact -- story-arc shape fits the load and carries implicit permission for natural compression over time

BECAUSE precision bar adopted -- a rationale is durable when a future agent in a new session could cite it back to the current human and win the argument on the strength of the rationale alone -- recorded in Getting-Started-For-Humans.txt as canonical

guardrail-against-self recognized and preserved as a product feature/control mechanism -- the rationale record exists in part to hold a future human to the standard a past human set when the two disagree -- protects against the generous-mood-human plus agreeable-agent failure mode in both directions
AGENTS-Arc piloted with the narrative schema in AI-Links itself -- storage path narratives/projects/ai-links.json plus narratives/register.json -- JSON output per schema mandate

in-session-capture-triggers (render-inversion rule A, self-inspection digest rule B) added to narrative schema meta as terrain -- paired with a UserPromptSubmit hook in .claude/settings.json at repo root as the harness companion -- schema describes the shape, hook enforces at the edge of agent action -- shipped as the first worked example of the schemas-describe-harnesses-enforce separation

narrative schema promoted from reactive-stepchild to load-bearing upstream schema -- the promotion came from three concrete shifts: gained in-session triggers, got a harness companion, found a second use-case (Arc) -- not marketing, structural role change -- the narrative schema now governs rationale for changes to the other three schemas in any deployment that adopts the Arc pattern

what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope role text tightened in AGENTS-schema-governance.json -- role-text-only change chosen over a rename (rename would have broken gov2gov-resilience for existing deployments) -- new role text uses affirmation-only language: scope-level invariant that holds across the continuing session loop, durable target, delineated from session-close by naming the sibling field agent-session-open-close-measurement rather than by saying 'NOT a session-end criterion' -- negation-by-exclusion rejected as weaker terrain -- manifest bumped 0.1.4 to 0.1.5 to record the new governance sha256

runtime provenance and workspace-role envelope implemented as a harness-layer feature, not an AGENTS schema change -- every install/schema/migration claim from the four core harness outputs must first declare the speaker's workspace role and runtime provenance -- user-profile plugin runtime remains useful as convenience runtime but is explicitly not source truth -- AI-Links/source-style workspaces do not receive consumer AGENTS materialization pressure unless explicitly requested -- gov2gov migration now emits post-migration inventory evidence instead of hard gates, preserving migration flexibility while making underdelivery visible
known-decisions.requested-by: Mike Herring; sharpened by external-agent review; implemented by Codex

known-decisions.decision: canonical provenance definition adopted for explanatory surfaces: 'provenance is the answer to who said what to whom, from where, using what source of truth, and why?' -- this is the human-native form Mike has wanted since early sessions but originally judged too token-expensive when imagined as narrative prose -- the compressed claim-label form (speaker, audience, claim, source-of-truth/confidence, reason-for-trust) is cheap enough to no longer trigger that concern and can be read through the d010 envelope without forcing every layer into identical fields -- the question line itself is the durable canon and belongs in Getting-Started or other explanatory surfaces; field naming follows from it
known-decisions.requested-by: Mike Herring (originally years ago in narrative-prose form, returned to in this session in compressed envelope form during the second-pass GPT-5.5 conversation captured in e034)

known-decisions.decision: narrative is the strongest durable home for provenance because it is where multi-speaker context muddles without explicit attribution -- live example named by Mike: 'GPT5.5 says one thing, you say another, I say yet another, I'm relaying, and it's easy to muddle the source' -- harness-layer provenance (d010) addresses a different problem (claim trust at the moment of harness output) and is shipped; schema-layer provenance is partial via manifest sha256 and has not yet shown observed failure pressure -- the symmetric three-layer formalization (typed speaker/audience/basis/disposition shapes across harness, schema, and narrative as a structural symmetry) is parked, not killed -- Mike's reasoning: 'elegance for a program is not the right test for contextual beings that misremember like humans and AI agents' -- formalization should follow observed failures, not structural symmetry -- design work for the narrative-layer surface is tracked as e036
known-decisions.requested-by: Mike Herring (corrected an earlier 'park as premature' read by naming the live multi-agent-relay example as observed need rather than theoretical symmetry)

known-decisions.decision: open threads are expected in a living product; the control problem is whether they are traceable, visible, and owned -- visible open threads are continuity infrastructure, while invisible open threads are residue that later agents misread or lose
known-decisions.requested-by: Mike Herring (corrected a summary that treated open threads themselves as the risk)

known-decisions.decision: ECC is a different product class and an inspiration surface; Anarchy-AI can aspire to complement ECC and systems like it -- the products are not partners or equals in the same relationship, they do different important things -- comparisons should learn from ECC's lifecycle maturity without collapsing Anarchy-AI into ECC's class or positioning
known-decisions.requested-by: Mike Herring (corrected the earlier relationship framing after the ECC comparison summary, then refined the relationship to complement rather than partnership)

known-decisions.decision: schemas cannot self-fulfill and plugin visibility is not harness truth -- schemas make terrain legible, but setup/runtime/proof lanes must materialize and observe the state they describe; current Codex plugin incompatibility should be repaired as a host-adapter issue without letting it rewrite core harness source truth; the next harness maturity lane is install lifecycle discipline: declared install plans, target adapters, install-state ledger, doctor/status diagnostics, bounded repair, and validation
known-decisions.requested-by: Mike Herring (explicitly told Codex to ignore the stale plugin, respect scratchpad #1, and avoid adding rules/walls where careful language plus easier terrain is the actual product move)

good.description: pressure-test abstract ideas against a live artifact before committing to them -- the terms-to-skills proposal sounded coherent in the abstract and was defeated by ten minutes of reading the actual Workorders/AGENTS-Terms.md file -- any future schema-shape proposal should be evaluated against at least one live consuming artifact before it lands in a schema edit

name-with-load-attached -- when Mike asks the agent to name something (AGENTS-Arc, guardrail-against-self), the chosen name must carry the load the artifact does -- names that do not carry the load produce drift, names that do carry the load produce alignment -- the narrative schema output format choice (JSON not markdown) is an example: the name carried the constraint

ask for pushback when pushback is what is needed -- the explicit instruction 'Now is not the time to be overly agreeable' produced visibly different session output and caught at least two scope-creep moments that an agreeable agent would have ratified -- the instruction itself is a reusable capture pattern

first-contact external-agent evaluations are load-bearing product data, not noise to filter out -- when a fresh evaluator (canonical example: GPT-5.5 on 2026-04-23) reads the repo cold with no context correction, their findings measure what the pre-context shaping actually communicates to a newcomer -- the gap between 'what the repo meant' and 'what the cold reader heard' is the scratchpad's pre-context-steering hypothesis being tested in the wild -- this reframes first-contact data from noisy-to-discard into the primary measurement instrument for pre-context efficacy -- in the GPT-5.5 case the first-contact read named 'AI-Links carries two identities' as a risk, which on a stale-runtime second look got recast as a harness-scope-mismatch failure class (e027); both readings were true at different layers, and only the first-contact one could have surfaced the identity-ambiguity as an adopter-perceivable risk because the second pass had been told the intended framing -- Mike's note: first-contact evaluations are important and should be documented as a great learning opportunity -- operationalization tracked as e030

restraint-as-feature when consumers are contextual beings -- when the system will be consumed by humans and AI agents who misremember (rather than by programs), structural elegance and symmetry are the wrong correctness tests -- bolting on every idea that 'seems right at the time' produces internal coherence the consumers cannot hold -- canonical example from this session: declining the symmetric three-layer provenance formalization (harness/schema/narrative as a typed structural symmetry) even though the symmetry was clean, because only the narrative layer had observed pressure (d012) -- Mike's framing: 'we didn't get to magic by bolting on every idea that seemed right at the time -- it's elegant for a program, but not for contextual beings that misremember like humans and AI agents' -- this is the positive form of the existing terrain-default-promote-only-on-failure posture (e002, d005) lifted from individual schema features to whole-system additions -- a yes-rule for restraint, not just absence of a no-rule

bad.description: the glib BECAUSE -- rationale written as dismissal instead of precise reasoning fails the BECAUSE precision bar (a future agent could not cite it back and win the argument) -- the canonical example from this session was a shorthand 'skills sucks' framing used for the terms-to-skills rejection, which was later corrected (see entry e014) -- the actual defensible rationale is the one recorded in d001: skills are optional by default and designed for capability discovery, terms are load-bearing by reference and depend on block-read cross-references, so converting fragments the ontology -- the pattern captured here is the shorthand, not any stance on skills as a feature -- skills are good, just not the right tool for terms specifically

agent-in-front-of-generous-mood-human reopens closed decisions -- the agent does not know the decision was closed for a specific reason, the human has lost the reason, the reframe sounds reasonable and the direction quietly reverses -- this failure is what guardrail-against-self / durable BECAUSE clauses exist to prevent -- recorded so the pattern stays visible

shipment-session enforcement gap -- when a schema rule is added in the same session in which it becomes shippable, the paired harness hook does not fire until the next session starts -- this creates a window in which the rule is in effect but unenforced, and the agent that authored the rule must self-enforce it on the very next turn -- observed this session: A+B shipped, next turn rendered a four-item list without materializing to Arc, Mike caught it before the agent did -- the pattern is not an argument against shipping rules and hooks together, it is a reminder that the authoring session is the hardest session to follow the rule in, because the agent's attention is on shipment not on the rule's behavioral demand

harness-applying-consumer-adoption-checks-to-authoring-repo -- observed when a schema-governance harness installed from the user profile was invoked inside AI-Links (the authoring and distribution repo for that same harness) and reported the root AGENTS family as copied_only because AGENTS-hello.md, AGENTS-Terms.md, AGENTS-Vision.md, AGENTS-Rules.md are absent -- they are absent on purpose because AI-Links is an authoring/distribution repo, not a consumer workspace that has adopted the family -- the harness had no repo-role distinction and no runtime provenance in its output, so it could not tell it was applying consumer-adoption checks to its own source of truth -- the failure was not a repo gap, it was an observation gap created by the harness acting on the wrong repo role from a stale installation lane -- this is kin to shipment-session enforcement gap and to the gov2gov post-migration verification gap (e025): all three reduce to 'the harness must declare its own scope, role, and provenance before its claims are trustworthy' -- fixed for e027 by d010/e032; kept here so future sessions can cite the resolved failure class when similar scope/provenance mistakes recur

handoff-note: next session should read this Arc record before reading the scratchpads or pen test -- the scratchpads were inputs to the decisions recorded here, not replacements for them -- open threads are e012, e013, e016-e019, e025, e028-e030, e035, and e036; they are parked intentionally, not forgotten, and d013 clarifies that visible open threads are continuity infrastructure rather than a cleanliness failure -- e011 (TheLinks sync) is closed by e033 with commit 7e70530 on TheLinks branch governance-schema -- e027 is closed by d010/e032 and e025 is now evidence-assisted by post_migration_inventory but still needs the WO2/TheLinks-style history investigation before it fully closes -- d011 (canonical provenance definition) and d012 (narrative is the strongest durable home for provenance) are this session's deepest theory shifts; e035 is the small concrete vocabulary upgrade derived from them, e036 is the design surface that should not be touched until observed muddling pulls on it -- restraint-as-feature is a load-bearing posture (observed-patterns.good); future sessions facing 'should we add layer X for symmetry' should re-read it before deciding -- ECC positioning is corrected by d014: ECC is a different product class and an inspiration surface; Anarchy-AI can aspire to complement ECC and systems like it by providing proof/provenance substrate rather than competing in ECC's lifecycle class or implying partnership/equality -- d015/e039 are the harness boundary correction: schemas cannot self-fulfill, plugin visibility is adapter evidence not source truth, and the next harness maturity lane is install lifecycle discipline rather than stronger prose walls -- the terms-to-skills walk-back is the canonical example of why schema-shape proposals must be pressure-tested against a live artifact before landing -- 'skills sucks' is the canonical example of a BECAUSE clause that fails the precision bar, kept in observed-patterns.bad for that reason
